1234	  // cross-upload contradictions (where claimA and claimB come from

1236	  // Conclusions has already been orphan-deleted.

1248	  resolvedAt   DateTime?

1252	
1253	  @@index([organizationId])
1254	  @@index([sourceUploadId])
1255	  @@index([status])
1256	}

1	"""
2	External literature ingestion — connectors produce ``Artifact`` + ``Chunk`` rows,
3	``Claim`` rows with ``claim_origin=literature`` (first author as ``Voice``).
4	
5	Copyright: respect ``license_status``; full text is only stored when access is lawful.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	import hashlib
11	import json
12	import re
13	import tempfile
14	import uuid
15	import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
16	from dataclasses import dataclass
17	from datetime import date, datetime, timezone
18	from pathlib import Path
19	from typing import Any, Optional, Protocol, runtime_checkable
20	
21	import requests
22	
23	from noosphere.ids import artifact_id_from_bytes, chunk_id
24	from noosphere.models import Artifact, Chunk, Claim, ClaimOrigin, ClaimType, InputSourceType, Speaker
25	from noosphere.observability import get_logger
26	from noosphere.store import Store
27	from noosphere.voices import ensure_voice_profile, register_voice_corpus_artifact
28	
29	logger = get_logger(__name__)
30	
31	NS = {"a": "http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom"}
32	
33	
34	@runtime_checkable
35	class SourceConnector(Protocol):
36	    name: str
37	
38	    def ingest(self, store: Store, **kwargs: Any) -> list[str]:
39	        """Return list of new artifact ids."""
40	        ...
41	
42	
43	def _first_author(author_field: str) -> str:
44	    s = (author_field or "").strip()
45	    if not s:
46	        return "Unknown author"
47	    part = re.split(r"[,;]|\band\b", s, maxsplit=1, flags=re.I)[0].strip()
48	    return part or "Unknown author"
49	
50	
51	def _section_type_for_paragraph(para: str) -> str:
52	    u = para.strip()[:80].upper()
53	    if u.startswith("ABSTRACT"):
54	        return "abstract"
55	    if u.startswith("INTRODUCTION") or u.startswith("1 INTRODUCTION"):
56	        return "introduction"
57	    if "METHOD" in u[:40]:
58	        return "methods"
59	    if "RESULT" in u[:40]:
60	        return "results"
61	    if "CONCLUSION" in u[:40]:
62	        return "conclusion"
63	    if "REFERENCE" in u[:40]:
64	        return "references"
65	    return "body"
66	
67	
68	def extract_pdf_text(path: Path) -> str:
69	    try:
70	        from pypdf import PdfReader
71	    except ImportError as e:  # pragma: no cover
72	        raise RuntimeError("pypdf is required for PDF ingestion") from e
73	    reader = PdfReader(str(path))
74	    parts: list[str] = []
75	    for page in reader.pages:
76	        t = page.extract_text() or ""
77	        if t.strip():
78	            parts.append(t)
79	    return "\n\n".join(parts)
80	
81	
82	def _chunks_from_text(
83	    text: str,
84	    artifact_id: str,
85	    *,
86	    max_chars: int = 1800,
87	) -> list[Chunk]:
88	    chunks: list[Chunk] = []
89	    offset = 0
90	    for para in re.split(r"\n\s*\n+", text):
91	        p = para.strip()
92	        if len(p) < 80:
93	            offset += len(para) + 2
94	            continue
95	        section = _section_type_for_paragraph(p)
96	        while p:
97	            piece = p[:max_chars]
98	            p = p[max_chars:]
99	            start = offset
100	            end = start + len(piece)
101	            cid = chunk_id(artifact_id, start, end)
102	            chunks.append(
103	                Chunk(
104	                    id=cid,
105	                    artifact_id=artifact_id,
106	                    start_offset=start,
107	                    end_offset=end,
108	                    text=piece,
109	                    metadata={"section_type": section},
110	                )
111	            )
112	            offset = end + 2
113	        offset += 2
114	    return chunks
115	
116	
117	def _claims_from_chunks(
118	    store: Store,
119	    chunks: list[Chunk],
120	    *,
121	    voice_id: str,
122	    voice_name: str,
123	    artifact_id: str,
124	    pub_date: date,
125	) -> int:
126	    spk = Speaker(name=voice_name, role="author")
127	    n = 0
128	    for ch in chunks:
129	        if len(ch.text.strip()) < 60:
130	            continue
131	        cid = f"lit_{artifact_id[:8]}_{ch.id}_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}"
132	        c = Claim(
133	            id=cid,
134	            text=ch.text.strip()[:4000],
135	            speaker=spk,
136	            episode_id=f"literature:{artifact_id}",
137	            episode_date=pub_date,
138	            claim_type=ClaimType.METHODOLOGICAL,
139	            chunk_id=ch.id,
140	            segment_context=ch.text[:500],
141	            claim_origin=ClaimOrigin.LITERATURE,
142	            voice_id=voice_id,
143	            source_type=InputSourceType.EXTERNAL,
144	            source_id=artifact_id,
145	            source_span_start=ch.start_offset,
146	            source_span_end=ch.end_offset,
147	        )
148	        store.put_claim(c)
149	        n += 1
150	    return n
151	
152	
153	@dataclass
154	class LiteratureIngestResult:
155	    artifact_id: str
156	    voice_id: str
157	    claims_written: int
158	
159	
160	def ingest_literature_text(
161	    store: Store,
162	    *,
163	    title: str,
164	    author: str,
165	    body: str,
166	    connector: str,
167	    license_status: str,
168	    pub_date: Optional[date] = None,
169	    uri: str = "",
170	    mime_type: str = "text/plain",
171	) -> LiteratureIngestResult:
172	    raw = body.encode("utf-8", errors="replace")
173	    if not raw.strip():
174	        raw = f"{title}|{uri}|{connector}".encode("utf-8", errors="replace")
175	    aid = artifact_id_from_bytes(raw)
176	    voice_name = _first_author(author)
177	    voice = ensure_voice_profile(store, voice_name, copyright_status=f"literature:{connector}")
178	    voice = register_voice_corpus_artifact(store, voice, aid)
179	    pd = pub_date or date.today()
180	    art = Artifact(
181	        id=aid,
182	        uri=uri or f"literature:{connector}:{aid}",
183	        mime_type=mime_type,
184	        byte_length=len(raw),
185	        content_sha256=hashlib.sha256(raw).hexdigest(),
186	        title=title,
187	        author=author,
188	        source_date=pd,
189	        license_status=license_status,
190	        literature_connector=connector,
191	    )
192	    store.put_artifact(art)
193	    chunks = _chunks_from_text(body, aid)
194	    for ch in chunks:
195	        store.put_chunk(ch)
196	    n = _claims_from_chunks(store, chunks, voice_id=voice.id, voice_name=voice.canonical_name, artifact_id=aid, pub_date=pd)
197	    logger.info("literature_ingested", artifact_id=aid, connector=connector, claims=n)
198	    return LiteratureIngestResult(aid, voice.id, n)
199	
200	
201	class LocalPDFConnector:
202	    name = "local_pdf"
203	
204	    def ingest(
205	        self,
206	        store: Store,
207	        *,
208	        path: str | Path,
209	        license_status: str = "firm_licensed",
210	        title: str = "",
211	        author: str = "",
212	        pub_date: Optional[date] = None,
213	    ) -> list[str]:
214	        p = Path(path)
215	        text = extract_pdf_text(p)
216	        if license_status == "restricted_metadata_only":
217	            text = text[:0]
218	        meta_path = p.with_suffix(".json")
219	        if meta_path.is_file():
220	            try:
221	                meta = json.loads(meta_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
222	                title = str(meta.get("title", title or p.stem))
223	                author = str(meta.get("author", author or ""))
224	                if meta.get("date"):
225	                    pub_date = date.fromisoformat(str(meta["date"])[:10])
226	            except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):
227	                pass
228	        if not title:
229	            title = p.stem
230	        res = ingest_literature_text(
231	            store,
232	            title=title,
233	            author=author or "Unknown",
234	            body=text or "(no extractable text — add OCR or text layer)",
235	            connector=self.name,
236	            license_status=license_status,
237	            pub_date=pub_date,
238	            uri=str(p.resolve()),
239	            mime_type="application/pdf",
240	        )
241	        return [res.artifact_id]
242	
243	
244	class ManualConnector:
245	    name = "manual"
246	
247	    def ingest(
248	        self,
249	        store: Store,
250	        *,
251	        path: str | Path,
252	        license_status: str = "firm_licensed",
253	        title: str = "",
254	        author: str = "",
255	        pub_date: Optional[date] = None,
256	    ) -> list[str]:
257	        p = Path(path)
258	        suf = p.suffix.lower()
259	        if suf == ".pdf":
260	            return LocalPDFConnector().ingest(
261	                store, path=p, license_status=license_status, title=title, author=author, pub_date=pub_date
262	            )
263	        body = p.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
264	        res = ingest_literature_text(
265	            store,
266	            title=title or p.stem,
267	            author=author or "Unknown",
268	            body=body,
269	            connector=self.name,
270	            license_status=license_status,
271	            pub_date=pub_date,
272	            uri=str(p.resolve()),
273	            mime_type="text/markdown" if suf in {".md", ".markdown"} else "text/plain",
274	        )
275	        return [res.artifact_id]
276	
277	
278	class ArxivConnector:
279	    name = "arxiv"
280	
281	    def ingest(
282	        self,
283	        store: Store,
284	        *,
285	        search_query: str = "cat:physics.soc-ph",
286	        max_results: int = 5,
287	        full_text: bool = False,
288	    ) -> list[str]:
289	        """Fetch arXiv Atom API; stores abstract (and optional PDF text when ``full_text``)."""
290	        url = "https://export.arxiv.org/api/query"
291	        r = requests.get(
292	            url,
293	            params={"search_query": search_query, "start": 0, "max_results": max_results},
294	            timeout=60,
295	        )
296	        r.raise_for_status()
297	        root = ET.fromstring(r.content)
298	        ids_out: list[str] = []
299	        for entry in root.findall("a:entry", NS):
300	            title_el = entry.find("a:title", NS)
301	            title = (title_el.text or "").strip().replace("\n", " ")
302	            published = entry.find("a:published", NS)
303	            pd: Optional[date] = None
304	            if published is not None and published.text:
305	                try:
306	                    pd = date.fromisoformat(published.text[:10])
307	                except ValueError:
308	                    pd = None
309	            authors = [a.find("a:name", NS).text or "" for a in entry.findall("a:author", NS) if a.find("a:name", NS) is not None]
310	            author = "; ".join(authors) if authors else ""
311	            summary_el = entry.find("a:summary", NS)
312	            summary = (summary_el.text or "").strip() if summary_el is not None else ""
313	            id_el = entry.find("a:id", NS)
314	            arxiv_url = (id_el.text or "").strip() if id_el is not None else ""
315	            body = f"URL: {arxiv_url}\n\nAbstract:\n{summary}"
316	            if full_text and arxiv_url:
317	                m = re.search(r"arxiv\.org/abs/([^/]+)", arxiv_url)
318	                if m:
319	                    aid_pdf = m.group(1)
320	                    pdf_url = f"https://arxiv.org/pdf/{aid_pdf}.pdf"
321	                    try:
322	                        pr = requests.get(pdf_url, timeout=120)
323	                        pr.raise_for_status()
324	                        tmp = Path(tempfile.gettempdir()) / f"arxiv_{aid_pdf}.pdf"
325	                        tmp.write_bytes(pr.content)
326	                        body = extract_pdf_text(tmp)
327	                    except Exception as e:
328	                        logger.warning("arxiv_pdf_fetch_failed", error=str(e))
329	            res = ingest_literature_text(
330	                store,
331	                title=title or "arXiv item",
332	                author=author,
333	                body=body,
334	                connector=self.name,
335	                license_status="open_access",
336	                pub_date=pd,
337	                uri=arxiv_url,
338	                mime_type="application/pdf" if full_text else "text/plain",
339	            )
340	            ids_out.append(res.artifact_id)
341	        return ids_out
342	
343	
344	class PhilPapersConnector:
345	    name = "philpapers"
346	
347	    def ingest(self, store: Store, *, query: str = "", max_items: int = 5, api_key: str = "") -> list[str]:
348	        key = api_key or __import__("os").environ.get("PHILPAPERS_API_KEY", "")
349	        if not key:
350	            logger.warning("philpapers_no_api_key", hint="Set PHILPAPERS_API_KEY for live search.")
351	            return []
352	        logger.warning("philpapers_stub", query=query, max_items=max_items)
353	        return []
354

"""
External literature ingestion — connectors produce ``Artifact`` + ``Chunk`` rows,
``Claim`` rows with ``claim_origin=literature`` (first author as ``Voice``).

Copyright: respect ``license_status``; full text is only stored when access is lawful.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import hashlib
import json
import re
import tempfile
import uuid
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import date, datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Optional, Protocol, runtime_checkable

import requests

from noosphere.ids import artifact_id_from_bytes, chunk_id
from noosphere.models import Artifact, Chunk, Claim, ClaimOrigin, ClaimType, InputSourceType, Speaker
from noosphere.observability import get_logger
from noosphere.store import Store
from noosphere.voices import ensure_voice_profile, register_voice_corpus_artifact

logger = get_logger(__name__)

NS = {"a": "http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom"}


@runtime_checkable
class SourceConnector(Protocol):
    name: str

    def ingest(self, store: Store, **kwargs: Any) -> list[str]:
        """Return list of new artifact ids."""
        ...


def _first_author(author_field: str) -> str:
    s = (author_field or "").strip()
    if not s:
        return "Unknown author"
    part = re.split(r"[,;]|\band\b", s, maxsplit=1, flags=re.I)[0].strip()
    return part or "Unknown author"


def _section_type_for_paragraph(para: str) -> str:
    u = para.strip()[:80].upper()
    if u.startswith("ABSTRACT"):
        return "abstract"
    if u.startswith("INTRODUCTION") or u.startswith("1 INTRODUCTION"):
        return "introduction"
    if "METHOD" in u[:40]:
        return "methods"
    if "RESULT" in u[:40]:
        return "results"
    if "CONCLUSION" in u[:40]:
        return "conclusion"
    if "REFERENCE" in u[:40]:
        return "references"
    return "body"


def extract_pdf_text(path: Path) -> str:
    try:
        from pypdf import PdfReader
    except ImportError as e:  # pragma: no cover
        raise RuntimeError("pypdf is required for PDF ingestion") from e
    reader = PdfReader(str(path))
    parts: list[str] = []
    for page in reader.pages:
        t = page.extract_text() or ""
        if t.strip():
            parts.append(t)
    return "\n\n".join(parts)


def _chunks_from_text(
    text: str,
    artifact_id: str,
    *,
    max_chars: int = 1800,
) -> list[Chunk]:
    chunks: list[Chunk] = []
    offset = 0
    for para in re.split(r"\n\s*\n+", text):
        p = para.strip()
        if len(p) < 80:
            offset += len(para) + 2
            continue
        section = _section_type_for_paragraph(p)
        while p:
            piece = p[:max_chars]
            p = p[max_chars:]
            start = offset
            end = start + len(piece)
            cid = chunk_id(artifact_id, start, end)
            chunks.append(
                Chunk(
                    id=cid,
                    artifact_id=artifact_id,
                    start_offset=start,
                    end_offset=end,
                    text=piece,
                    metadata={"section_type": section},
                )
            )
            offset = end + 2
        offset += 2
    return chunks


def _claims_from_chunks(
    store: Store,
    chunks: list[Chunk],
    *,
    voice_id: str,
    voice_name: str,
    artifact_id: str,
    pub_date: date,
) -> int:
    spk = Speaker(name=voice_name, role="author")
    n = 0
    for ch in chunks:
        if len(ch.text.strip()) < 60:
            continue
        cid = f"lit_{artifact_id[:8]}_{ch.id}_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}"
        c = Claim(
            id=cid,
            text=ch.text.strip()[:4000],
            speaker=spk,
            episode_id=f"literature:{artifact_id}",
            episode_date=pub_date,
            claim_type=ClaimType.METHODOLOGICAL,
            chunk_id=ch.id,
            segment_context=ch.text[:500],
            claim_origin=ClaimOrigin.LITERATURE,
            voice_id=voice_id,
            source_type=InputSourceType.EXTERNAL,
            source_id=artifact_id,
            source_span_start=ch.start_offset,
            source_span_end=ch.end_offset,
        )
        store.put_claim(c)
        n += 1
    return n


@dataclass
class LiteratureIngestResult:
    artifact_id: str
    voice_id: str
    claims_written: int


def ingest_literature_text(
    store: Store,
    *,
    title: str,
    author: str,
    body: str,
    connector: str,
    license_status: str,
    pub_date: Optional[date] = None,
    uri: str = "",
    mime_type: str = "text/plain",
) -> LiteratureIngestResult:
    raw = body.encode("utf-8", errors="replace")
    if not raw.strip():
        raw = f"{title}|{uri}|{connector}".encode("utf-8", errors="replace")
    aid = artifact_id_from_bytes(raw)
    voice_name = _first_author(author)
    voice = ensure_voice_profile(store, voice_name, copyright_status=f"literature:{connector}")
    voice = register_voice_corpus_artifact(store, voice, aid)
    pd = pub_date or date.today()
    art = Artifact(
        id=aid,
        uri=uri or f"literature:{connector}:{aid}",
        mime_type=mime_type,
        byte_length=len(raw),
        content_sha256=hashlib.sha256(raw).hexdigest(),
        title=title,
        author=author,
        source_date=pd,
        license_status=license_status,
        literature_connector=connector,
    )
    store.put_artifact(art)
    chunks = _chunks_from_text(body, aid)
    for ch in chunks:
        store.put_chunk(ch)
    n = _claims_from_chunks(store, chunks, voice_id=voice.id, voice_name=voice.canonical_name, artifact_id=aid, pub_date=pd)
    logger.info("literature_ingested", artifact_id=aid, connector=connector, claims=n)
    return LiteratureIngestResult(aid, voice.id, n)


class LocalPDFConnector:
    name = "local_pdf"

    def ingest(
        self,
        store: Store,
        *,
        path: str | Path,
        license_status: str = "firm_licensed",
        title: str = "",
        author: str = "",
        pub_date: Optional[date] = None,
    ) -> list[str]:
        p = Path(path)
        text = extract_pdf_text(p)
        if license_status == "restricted_metadata_only":
            text = text[:0]
        meta_path = p.with_suffix(".json")
        if meta_path.is_file():
            try:
                meta = json.loads(meta_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
                title = str(meta.get("title", title or p.stem))
                author = str(meta.get("author", author or ""))
                if meta.get("date"):
                    pub_date = date.fromisoformat(str(meta["date"])[:10])
            except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):
                pass
        if not title:
            title = p.stem
        res = ingest_literature_text(
            store,
            title=title,
            author=author or "Unknown",
            body=text or "(no extractable text — add OCR or text layer)",
            connector=self.name,
            license_status=license_status,
            pub_date=pub_date,
            uri=str(p.resolve()),
            mime_type="application/pdf",
        )
        return [res.artifact_id]


class ManualConnector:
    name = "manual"

    def ingest(
        self,
        store: Store,
        *,
        path: str | Path,
        license_status: str = "firm_licensed",
        title: str = "",
        author: str = "",
        pub_date: Optional[date] = None,
    ) -> list[str]:
        p = Path(path)
        suf = p.suffix.lower()
        if suf == ".pdf":
            return LocalPDFConnector().ingest(
                store, path=p, license_status=license_status, title=title, author=author, pub_date=pub_date
            )
        body = p.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
        res = ingest_literature_text(
            store,
            title=title or p.stem,
            author=author or "Unknown",
            body=body,
            connector=self.name,
            license_status=license_status,
            pub_date=pub_date,
            uri=str(p.resolve()),
            mime_type="text/markdown" if suf in {".md", ".markdown"} else "text/plain",
        )
        return [res.artifact_id]


class ArxivConnector:
    name = "arxiv"

    def ingest(
        self,
        store: Store,
        *,
        search_query: str = "cat:physics.soc-ph",
        max_results: int = 5,
        full_text: bool = False,
    ) -> list[str]:
        """Fetch arXiv Atom API; stores abstract (and optional PDF text when ``full_text``)."""
        url = "https://export.arxiv.org/api/query"
        r = requests.get(
            url,
            params={"search_query": search_query, "start": 0, "max_results": max_results},
            timeout=60,
        )
        r.raise_for_status()
        root = ET.fromstring(r.content)
        ids_out: list[str] = []
        for entry in root.findall("a:entry", NS):
            title_el = entry.find("a:title", NS)
            title = (title_el.text or "").strip().replace("\n", " ")
            published = entry.find("a:published", NS)
            pd: Optional[date] = None
            if published is not None and published.text:
                try:
                    pd = date.fromisoformat(published.text[:10])
                except ValueError:
                    pd = None
            authors = [a.find("a:name", NS).text or "" for a in entry.findall("a:author", NS) if a.find("a:name", NS) is not None]
            author = "; ".join(authors) if authors else ""
            summary_el = entry.find("a:summary", NS)
            summary = (summary_el.text or "").strip() if summary_el is not None else ""
            id_el = entry.find("a:id", NS)
            arxiv_url = (id_el.text or "").strip() if id_el is not None else ""
            body = f"URL: {arxiv_url}\n\nAbstract:\n{summary}"
            if full_text and arxiv_url:
                m = re.search(r"arxiv\.org/abs/([^/]+)", arxiv_url)
                if m:
                    aid_pdf = m.group(1)
                    pdf_url = f"https://arxiv.org/pdf/{aid_pdf}.pdf"
                    try:
                        pr = requests.get(pdf_url, timeout=120)
                        pr.raise_for_status()
                        tmp = Path(tempfile.gettempdir()) / f"arxiv_{aid_pdf}.pdf"
                        tmp.write_bytes(pr.content)
                        body = extract_pdf_text(tmp)
                    except Exception as e:
                        logger.warning("arxiv_pdf_fetch_failed", error=str(e))
            res = ingest_literature_text(
                store,
                title=title or "arXiv item",
                author=author,
                body=body,
                connector=self.name,
                license_status="open_access",
                pub_date=pd,
                uri=arxiv_url,
                mime_type="application/pdf" if full_text else "text/plain",
            )
            ids_out.append(res.artifact_id)
        return ids_out


class PhilPapersConnector:
    name = "philpapers"

    def ingest(self, store: Store, *, query: str = "", max_items: int = 5, api_key: str = "") -> list[str]:
        key = api_key or __import__("os").environ.get("PHILPAPERS_API_KEY", "")
        if not key:
            logger.warning("philpapers_no_api_key", hint="Set PHILPAPERS_API_KEY for live search.")
            return []
        logger.warning("philpapers_stub", query=query, max_items=max_items)
        return []

1	You are operating in /Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus.
2	
3	Goal: implement a real belief-revision engine — closer to AGM-style
4	revision than the current "edit a conclusion" flow. When new evidence
5	arrives that contradicts a published conclusion, the firm should be
6	able to compute the minimal-distance revision: which downstream
7	conclusions are affected, what the new entailments are, and what
8	remains stable.
9	
10	METHODOLOGICAL_REORIENTATION.md notes that the current Belief Revision
11	System is mostly "time replay, conclusion lineage, and reanalysis hooks,
12	but not a full AGM belief-revision implementation." This prompt is the
13	first concrete step toward that.
14	
15	Before you start — read:
16	1. `noosphere/noosphere/cascade/graph.py` — the firm's working belief
17	   graph primitive.
18	2. `noosphere/noosphere/cascade/traverse.py`.
19	3. `noosphere/noosphere/temporal.py` and `temporal_replay.py`.
20	4. The relations vocabulary in `noosphere/noosphere/models.py` —
21	   supports / contradicts / refines / instantiates.
22	
23	Implement:
24	
25	A. Define `RevisionInput`: a tuple (claim_id, new_evidence, evidence
26	   weight). Multiple inputs can be batched.
27	
28	B. Revision algorithm in
29	   `noosphere/noosphere/cascade/revision.py`:
30	   1. Mark the targeted claim with the new evidence.
31	   2. Re-run coherence on the local neighborhood.
32	   3. For every conclusion connected through `supports` /
33	      `refines` / `instantiates` to the targeted claim, recompute its
34	      composite confidence using the cascade weights.
35	   4. Output a `RevisionPlan`: conclusions whose confidence changed by
36	      more than δ, conclusions newly contradicted (entropic
37	      distance > θ), conclusions newly supported.
38	   The minimum-distance principle: prefer plans that change fewest
39	   downstream nodes when multiple revisions satisfy the new evidence.
40	
41	C. Operator UX. When a founder marks new evidence on a claim from the
42	   conclusion detail page, the UI shows a "Revision impact preview"
43	   modal listing every affected conclusion before the revision is
44	   committed. Confirm-to-commit; cancel reverts the marker.
45	
46	D. Audit trail. Every committed revision writes a `RevisionEvent` row
47	   with the input, the plan applied, and a snapshot of pre-revision
48	   confidence values. The ledger references this so the time-replay
49	   primitives can reconstruct prior states exactly.
50	
51	E. Public surface — narrow. Public articles whose underlying
52	   conclusion is revised get an "updated" pill linking to a
53	   `/revisions/<event-id>` page that shows the diff in plain prose
54	   ("we previously concluded X with confidence Y; new evidence Z has
55	   changed the conclusion to ...").
56	
57	F. Tests on a synthetic graph: revise a deep node, assert the correct
58	   set of ancestors is impacted; revise to a contradicting state,
59	   assert at most one minimum-distance plan; assert the revert path.
60	
61	Constraints
62	- The revision algorithm must be deterministic: same inputs and same
63	  graph snapshot always produce the same plan. Tie-breaks documented.
64	- A revision cannot silently delete a claim. Retracting a claim is a
65	  separate operation that itself produces a revision.
66	- The UI must NOT auto-commit a revision that affects more than K
67	  conclusions; above K it requires a typed confirmation.
68	
69	SCOPE
70	- `noosphere/noosphere/cascade/revision.py` CREATE
71	- `noosphere/noosphere/cascade/graph.py` MODIFY
72	- `noosphere/noosphere/cascade/__init__.py` MODIFY
73	- `noosphere/tests/test_revision.py` CREATE
74	- `theseus-codex/prisma/schema.prisma` MODIFY
75	- `theseus-codex/src/app/(authed)/conclusions/[id]/RevisionPreview.tsx` CREATE
76	- `theseus-codex/src/app/revisions/[id]/page.tsx` CREATE
77	- `theseus-codex/src/lib/revisionApi.ts` CREATE

You are operating in /Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus.

Goal: implement a real belief-revision engine — closer to AGM-style
revision than the current "edit a conclusion" flow. When new evidence
arrives that contradicts a published conclusion, the firm should be
able to compute the minimal-distance revision: which downstream
conclusions are affected, what the new entailments are, and what
remains stable.

METHODOLOGICAL_REORIENTATION.md notes that the current Belief Revision
System is mostly "time replay, conclusion lineage, and reanalysis hooks,
but not a full AGM belief-revision implementation." This prompt is the
first concrete step toward that.

Before you start — read:
1. `noosphere/noosphere/cascade/graph.py` — the firm's working belief
   graph primitive.
2. `noosphere/noosphere/cascade/traverse.py`.
3. `noosphere/noosphere/temporal.py` and `temporal_replay.py`.
4. The relations vocabulary in `noosphere/noosphere/models.py` —
   supports / contradicts / refines / instantiates.

Implement:

A. Define `RevisionInput`: a tuple (claim_id, new_evidence, evidence
   weight). Multiple inputs can be batched.

B. Revision algorithm in
   `noosphere/noosphere/cascade/revision.py`:
   1. Mark the targeted claim with the new evidence.
   2. Re-run coherence on the local neighborhood.
   3. For every conclusion connected through `supports` /
      `refines` / `instantiates` to the targeted claim, recompute its
      composite confidence using the cascade weights.
   4. Output a `RevisionPlan`: conclusions whose confidence changed by
      more than δ, conclusions newly contradicted (entropic
      distance > θ), conclusions newly supported.
   The minimum-distance principle: prefer plans that change fewest
   downstream nodes when multiple revisions satisfy the new evidence.

C. Operator UX. When a founder marks new evidence on a claim from the
   conclusion detail page, the UI shows a "Revision impact preview"
   modal listing every affected conclusion before the revision is
   committed. Confirm-to-commit; cancel reverts the marker.

D. Audit trail. Every committed revision writes a `RevisionEvent` row
   with the input, the plan applied, and a snapshot of pre-revision
   confidence values. The ledger references this so the time-replay
   primitives can reconstruct prior states exactly.

E. Public surface — narrow. Public articles whose underlying
   conclusion is revised get an "updated" pill linking to a
   `/revisions/<event-id>` page that shows the diff in plain prose
   ("we previously concluded X with confidence Y; new evidence Z has
   changed the conclusion to ...").

F. Tests on a synthetic graph: revise a deep node, assert the correct
   set of ancestors is impacted; revise to a contradicting state,
   assert at most one minimum-distance plan; assert the revert path.

Constraints
- The revision algorithm must be deterministic: same inputs and same
  graph snapshot always produce the same plan. Tie-breaks documented.
- A revision cannot silently delete a claim. Retracting a claim is a
  separate operation that itself produces a revision.
- The UI must NOT auto-commit a revision that affects more than K
  conclusions; above K it requires a typed confirmation.

SCOPE
- `noosphere/noosphere/cascade/revision.py` CREATE
- `noosphere/noosphere/cascade/graph.py` MODIFY
- `noosphere/noosphere/cascade/__init__.py` MODIFY
- `noosphere/tests/test_revision.py` CREATE
- `theseus-codex/prisma/schema.prisma` MODIFY
- `theseus-codex/src/app/(authed)/conclusions/[id]/RevisionPreview.tsx` CREATE
- `theseus-codex/src/app/revisions/[id]/page.tsx` CREATE
- `theseus-codex/src/lib/revisionApi.ts` CREATE

1	You are operating in /Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus.
2	
3	Goal: every objection produced by the peer-review swarm gets a severity
4	score. Severity = "would, if true, change the conclusion's confidence
5	by how much?" Right now objections accumulate flat; that means a
6	nitpick is treated like a structural blow. Severity-weighting fixes
7	that and feeds back into the MQS Severity sub-score.
8	
9	Before you start — read:
10	1. The peer-review tree (esp. `noosphere/noosphere/peer_review/`).
11	2. The MQS work (prompt 01).
12	3. `noosphere/noosphere/cascade/graph.py` for cascade weighting.
13	
14	Implement:
15	
16	A. Severity rubric. An objection's severity is computed from:
17	   - the cascade-weight of the supporting edge it attacks
18	   - the centrality of the attacked claim in the conclusion's
19	     argument graph (compute via existing cascade traversal)
20	   - whether the objection points to a high-severity matched
21	     failure mode (prompt 03)
22	   - the credibility of any source the objection invokes (prompt 19)
23	   - the LLM judge's own estimated severity, capped — the rubric
24	     does not let the model self-promote without grounding.
25	
26	B. Implement scorer in
27	   `noosphere/noosphere/peer_review/severity.py`. Output is
28	   {low, medium, high} plus a numeric value in [0,1]. Each
29	   objection is annotated.
30	
31	C. Weighted aggregation. Replace the flat "objection count" feeding
32	   MQS with a severity-weighted count and a max-severity gate. Two
33	   high-severity objections that both stand drop the conclusion's
34	   MQS Severity sub-score below the publish threshold; ten
35	   low-severity objections do not.
36	
37	D. UI. Reviewer queue
38	   (`(authed)/peer-review/`) sorts by severity, with a colored bar
39	   per objection. The conclusion detail page shows only the top-K
40	   severity objections by default with "show all" affordance.
41	
42	E. Author response loop. When an objection is annotated severity
43	   {high}, the firm cannot publish the conclusion until the
44	   reviewer or founder writes a response. Severity {medium}
45	   responses are encouraged; {low} are optional.
46	
47	F. Tests on synthetic objections of known structure. Assert: the
48	   severity is monotonic in cascade-weight; the max-severity gate
49	   triggers; the response-required flag fires for high.
50	
51	Constraints
52	- The LLM judge can never produce severity higher than the
53	  cascade/centrality input allows. The judge estimates within a
54	  bracket the rubric defines.
55	- A severity score expires when the conclusion is materially
56	  revised (revision engine, prompt 16). Stale severities are not
57	  carried forward.
58	
59	SCOPE
60	- `noosphere/noosphere/peer_review/severity.py` CREATE
61	- `noosphere/noosphere/peer_review/swarm.py` MODIFY
62	- `noosphere/noosphere/peer_review/reviewer.py` MODIFY
63	- `noosphere/noosphere/evaluation/mqs.py` MODIFY
64	- `noosphere/tests/test_objection_severity.py` CREATE
65	- `theseus-codex/src/app/(authed)/peer-review/page.tsx` MODIFY
66	- `theseus-codex/src/app/(authed)/conclusions/[id]/ObjectionList.tsx` CREATE

You are operating in /Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus.

Goal: every objection produced by the peer-review swarm gets a severity
score. Severity = "would, if true, change the conclusion's confidence
by how much?" Right now objections accumulate flat; that means a
nitpick is treated like a structural blow. Severity-weighting fixes
that and feeds back into the MQS Severity sub-score.

Before you start — read:
1. The peer-review tree (esp. `noosphere/noosphere/peer_review/`).
2. The MQS work (prompt 01).
3. `noosphere/noosphere/cascade/graph.py` for cascade weighting.

Implement:

A. Severity rubric. An objection's severity is computed from:
   - the cascade-weight of the supporting edge it attacks
   - the centrality of the attacked claim in the conclusion's
     argument graph (compute via existing cascade traversal)
   - whether the objection points to a high-severity matched
     failure mode (prompt 03)
   - the credibility of any source the objection invokes (prompt 19)
   - the LLM judge's own estimated severity, capped — the rubric
     does not let the model self-promote without grounding.

B. Implement scorer in
   `noosphere/noosphere/peer_review/severity.py`. Output is
   {low, medium, high} plus a numeric value in [0,1]. Each
   objection is annotated.

C. Weighted aggregation. Replace the flat "objection count" feeding
   MQS with a severity-weighted count and a max-severity gate. Two
   high-severity objections that both stand drop the conclusion's
   MQS Severity sub-score below the publish threshold; ten
   low-severity objections do not.

D. UI. Reviewer queue
   (`(authed)/peer-review/`) sorts by severity, with a colored bar
   per objection. The conclusion detail page shows only the top-K
   severity objections by default with "show all" affordance.

E. Author response loop. When an objection is annotated severity
   {high}, the firm cannot publish the conclusion until the
   reviewer or founder writes a response. Severity {medium}
   responses are encouraged; {low} are optional.

F. Tests on synthetic objections of known structure. Assert: the
   severity is monotonic in cascade-weight; the max-severity gate
   triggers; the response-required flag fires for high.

Constraints
- The LLM judge can never produce severity higher than the
  cascade/centrality input allows. The judge estimates within a
  bracket the rubric defines.
- A severity score expires when the conclusion is materially
  revised (revision engine, prompt 16). Stale severities are not
  carried forward.

SCOPE
- `noosphere/noosphere/peer_review/severity.py` CREATE
- `noosphere/noosphere/peer_review/swarm.py` MODIFY
- `noosphere/noosphere/peer_review/reviewer.py` MODIFY
- `noosphere/noosphere/evaluation/mqs.py` MODIFY
- `noosphere/tests/test_objection_severity.py` CREATE
- `theseus-codex/src/app/(authed)/peer-review/page.tsx` MODIFY
- `theseus-codex/src/app/(authed)/conclusions/[id]/ObjectionList.tsx` CREATE

1	"""Severity scoring for peer-review objections.
2	
3	Every objection produced by the swarm is annotated with a severity in
4	``{"low", "medium", "high"}`` and a numeric value in ``[0, 1]``. The
5	score answers a single question: *if this objection is true, how much
6	would it move the conclusion's confidence?*
7	
8	The rubric is composed of five inputs, deliberately mostly grounded
9	outside the LLM judge so the model cannot self-promote a nitpick into
10	a structural blow:
11	
12	1. ``cascade_weight`` — confidence of the supporting edge the objection
13	   attacks. A 0.9 supports edge under attack matters more than a 0.3 one.
14	2. ``claim_centrality`` — how central the attacked claim is in the
15	   conclusion's argument graph (downstream-edge fan-out, normalised).
16	   An attack on a load-bearing premise outranks one on a tangent.
17	3. ``failure_mode_severity`` — if the objection points to a curated
18	   failure mode (see :mod:`noosphere.methods.failure_modes`), the
19	   curated severity is folded in. Curated `high` failure modes carry
20	   strong weight because a human approved them.
21	4. ``source_credibility`` — when the objection cites a source, the
22	   source's Beta-credibility posterior modulates the weight. A
23	   tabloid-cited objection cannot land at high.
24	5. ``judge_severity`` — the LLM judge's own estimate, **capped** by a
25	   bracket the structural inputs above define. The judge picks within a
26	   range; it cannot promote past it.
27	
28	The bracket cap is the discipline: an objection's max severity is set
29	by the structural inputs; the judge can place inside that bracket but
30	not above. This is what makes the score robust to model puffery.
31	
32	A severity score expires when the underlying conclusion is materially
33	revised (revision engine, prompt 16). Stale scores are not carried
34	forward — see :func:`is_stale`.
35	"""
36	
37	from __future__ import annotations
38	
39	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
40	from datetime import datetime, timezone
41	from typing import Iterable, Literal, Optional, Protocol
42	
43	SeverityLabel = Literal["low", "medium", "high"]
44	
45	# Public bracket boundaries. Documented in the module docstring; the
46	# tests in `test_objection_severity.py` pin them.
47	LOW_MAX = 0.34
48	MEDIUM_MAX = 0.67  # >= MEDIUM_MAX is "high"
49	
50	
51	def _clamp01(x: float) -> float:
52	    if x != x:  # NaN
53	        return 0.0
54	    return max(0.0, min(1.0, float(x)))
55	
56	
57	@dataclass(frozen=True)
58	class SeverityInputs:
59	    """The structural inputs that bound an objection's severity.
60	
61	    All numeric fields are in [0, 1]. Callers fill what they have; a
62	    missing input contributes 0 (i.e. nothing) rather than skewing the
63	    bracket. The judge's estimate is provided separately so the rubric
64	    can cap it explicitly.
65	    """
66	
67	    cascade_weight: float = 0.0
68	    claim_centrality: float = 0.0
69	    failure_mode_severity: float = 0.0  # 0=no match, low=0.33, med=0.67, high=1.0
70	    source_credibility: Optional[float] = None  # None = no source cited
71	    judge_severity: Optional[float] = None  # None = no LLM estimate
72	
73	
74	@dataclass(frozen=True)
75	class ObjectionSeverity:
76	    """The severity annotation attached to one objection."""
77	
78	    value: float
79	    label: SeverityLabel
80	    bracket_floor: float
81	    bracket_ceiling: float
82	    inputs: SeverityInputs
83	    judge_capped: bool
84	    rationale: str = ""
85	    scored_at: datetime = field(
86	        default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc)
87	    )
88	    # Set when `mark_stale` runs after a material revision.
89	    stale: bool = False
90	
91	    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
92	        return {
93	            "value": round(self.value, 4),
94	            "label": self.label,
95	            "bracket_floor": round(self.bracket_floor, 4),
96	            "bracket_ceiling": round(self.bracket_ceiling, 4),
97	            "judge_capped": self.judge_capped,
98	            "stale": self.stale,
99	            "rationale": self.rationale,
100	            "inputs": {
101	                "cascade_weight": round(self.inputs.cascade_weight, 4),
102	                "claim_centrality": round(self.inputs.claim_centrality, 4),
103	                "failure_mode_severity": round(
104	                    self.inputs.failure_mode_severity, 4
105	                ),
106	                "source_credibility": (
107	                    None
108	                    if self.inputs.source_credibility is None
109	                    else round(self.inputs.source_credibility, 4)
110	                ),
111	                "judge_severity": (
112	                    None
113	                    if self.inputs.judge_severity is None
114	                    else round(self.inputs.judge_severity, 4)
115	                ),
116	            },
117	            "scored_at": self.scored_at.isoformat(),
118	        }
119	
120	
121	# ── Severity rubric ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
122	
123	
124	# Weights applied to the structural inputs to compute the bracket
125	# ceiling. Cascade-weight and centrality are the two primary structural
126	# drivers — together they account for the bulk of the budget. The
127	# failure-mode signal is curated, so it carries real weight too. Source
128	# credibility is a multiplicative modulation when present, not an
129	# additive driver — a low-credibility source caps the bracket from
130	# above. The weights are tuned so that:
131	#   * cascade=1, centrality=1, failure_mode=0 -> ceiling ~0.85 (allows
132	#     high if the judge sees a structural blow).
133	#   * cascade=0, centrality=0, failure_mode=0 -> ceiling = 0 (the judge
134	#     cannot promote a nitpick into anything).
135	_W_CASCADE = 0.40
136	_W_CENTRALITY = 0.30
137	_W_FAILURE = 0.30
138	
139	
140	def _structural_ceiling(inp: SeverityInputs) -> float:
141	    """Upper bound on severity from structural inputs alone."""
142	
143	    raw = (
144	        _W_CASCADE * _clamp01(inp.cascade_weight)
145	        + _W_CENTRALITY * _clamp01(inp.claim_centrality)
146	        + _W_FAILURE * _clamp01(inp.failure_mode_severity)
147	    )
148	    # Source credibility only modulates *down*: a present-but-weak
149	    # source lowers the ceiling; a present-and-strong source leaves it
150	    # alone; a missing source leaves it alone (we don't penalise an
151	    # objection for not citing a source — many objections are purely
152	    # methodological).
153	    cred = inp.source_credibility
154	    if cred is not None:
155	        raw = raw * _clamp01(cred)
156	    return _clamp01(raw)
157	
158	
159	def _structural_floor(inp: SeverityInputs) -> float:
160	    """Minimum severity from structural inputs.
161	
162	    The floor catches the case where the structural signal is so strong
163	    (e.g. curated `high` failure mode on a load-bearing claim) that
164	    even a meek LLM estimate should not pull the score down to "low".
165	    """
166	
167	    # The floor is the failure-mode signal when it lands in the high
168	    # bracket *and* the cascade/centrality structural support is
169	    # non-trivial. Otherwise no floor — the judge can park anywhere
170	    # within [0, ceiling].
171	    structural_support = _clamp01(
172	        0.5 * inp.cascade_weight + 0.5 * inp.claim_centrality
173	    )
174	    if inp.failure_mode_severity >= MEDIUM_MAX and structural_support >= 0.4:
175	        # Floor at the bottom of the medium bracket — never below
176	        # `low` if the curated prior is high and the claim has
177	        # structural weight.
178	        return LOW_MAX
179	    return 0.0
180	
181	
182	def label_for(value: float) -> SeverityLabel:
183	    if value < LOW_MAX:
184	        return "low"
185	    if value < MEDIUM_MAX:
186	        return "medium"
187	    return "high"
188	
189	
190	def score_objection(
191	    inputs: SeverityInputs, *, rationale: str = ""
192	) -> ObjectionSeverity:
193	    """Compute an :class:`ObjectionSeverity` from the rubric inputs.
194	
195	    The judge's estimate is clamped into ``[floor, ceiling]``. If it is
196	    None, the severity falls back to the ceiling (the structural inputs
197	    define the score on their own). This guarantees:
198	
199	    * Severity is monotonic in ``cascade_weight`` (and centrality, and
200	      failure-mode severity), holding other inputs fixed — see tests.
201	    * The judge cannot promote a low-bracket objection into "high".
202	    * A missing source is treated charitably (no penalty), but a
203	      present-and-weak source caps the ceiling.
204	    """
205	
206	    ceiling = _structural_ceiling(inputs)
207	    floor = min(_structural_floor(inputs), ceiling)
208	
209	    judge = inputs.judge_severity
210	    capped = False
211	    if judge is None:
212	        value = ceiling
213	    else:
214	        j = _clamp01(judge)
215	        value = j
216	        if value > ceiling:
217	            value = ceiling
218	            capped = True
219	        if value < floor:
220	            value = floor
221	            # Promoting up to floor is *not* a "judge cap" event — the
222	            # judge can disagree downwards but the structural prior
223	            # holds. We still record it on the rationale below.
224	
225	    return ObjectionSeverity(
226	        value=value,
227	        label=label_for(value),
228	        bracket_floor=floor,
229	        bracket_ceiling=ceiling,
230	        inputs=inputs,
231	        judge_capped=capped,
232	        rationale=rationale,
233	    )
234	
235	
236	def mark_stale(severity: ObjectionSeverity) -> ObjectionSeverity:
237	    """Return a copy of ``severity`` with ``stale=True``.
238	
239	    Called by the revision engine when the conclusion is materially
240	    revised — the old severity is no longer load-bearing because the
241	    underlying argument graph may have shifted.
242	    """
243	
244	    return ObjectionSeverity(
245	        value=severity.value,
246	        label=severity.label,
247	        bracket_floor=severity.bracket_floor,
248	        bracket_ceiling=severity.bracket_ceiling,
249	        inputs=severity.inputs,
250	        judge_capped=severity.judge_capped,
251	        rationale=severity.rationale,
252	        scored_at=severity.scored_at,
253	        stale=True,
254	    )
255	
256	
257	def is_stale(severity: ObjectionSeverity) -> bool:
258	    return severity.stale
259	
260	
261	# ── Aggregation: weighted count + max-severity gate ─────────────────
262	
263	
264	@dataclass(frozen=True)
265	class SeverityAggregate:
266	    """Aggregate of a swarm pass's per-objection severities.
267	
268	    Fed into the MQS Severity sub-score and the publication gate.
269	    """
270	
271	    weighted_count: float
272	    max_value: float
273	    max_label: SeverityLabel
274	    high_count: int
275	    medium_count: int
276	    low_count: int
277	    objections_total: int
278	    blocking: bool  # >= 2 unresolved high or any single high+blocker
279	    response_required_high: bool
280	    response_recommended_medium: bool
281	
282	    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
283	        return {
284	            "weighted_count": round(self.weighted_count, 4),
285	            "max_value": round(self.max_value, 4),
286	            "max_label": self.max_label,
287	            "high_count": self.high_count,
288	            "medium_count": self.medium_count,
289	            "low_count": self.low_count,
290	            "objections_total": self.objections_total,
291	            "blocking": self.blocking,
292	            "response_required_high": self.response_required_high,
293	            "response_recommended_medium": self.response_recommended_medium,
294	        }
295	
296	
297	# Two unresolved high-severity objections trip the publication gate.
298	# The number is intentional — one outlier high might be a single
299	# reviewer overshooting; two distinct high objections is a structural
300	# pattern. Documented in tests.
301	HIGH_BLOCK_COUNT = 2
302	
303	
304	def aggregate(
305	    severities: Iterable[ObjectionSeverity],
306	    *,
307	    responses_resolved_for: Optional[set[int]] = None,
308	) -> SeverityAggregate:
309	    """Aggregate severities into the MQS-feeding signal.
310	
311	    ``responses_resolved_for`` is an optional set of severity indices
312	    that have been answered by a reviewer/founder rebuttal. Only
313	    *unresolved* high severities count toward the blocking gate — the
314	    response loop is the firm's escape hatch.
315	
316	    Stale severities are ignored entirely. The revision engine clears
317	    them by calling :func:`mark_stale`; an aggregate computed after a
318	    revision treats those as if they were never produced.
319	    """
320	
321	    items = [s for s in severities if not s.stale]
322	    if not items:
323	        return SeverityAggregate(
324	            weighted_count=0.0,
325	            max_value=0.0,
326	            max_label="low",
327	            high_count=0,
328	            medium_count=0,
329	            low_count=0,
330	            objections_total=0,
331	            blocking=False,
332	            response_required_high=False,
333	            response_recommended_medium=False,
334	        )
335	
336	    resolved = responses_resolved_for or set()
337	    weighted = sum(s.value for s in items)
338	    max_v = max(s.value for s in items)
339	    high = sum(1 for s in items if s.label == "high")
340	    medium = sum(1 for s in items if s.label == "medium")
341	    low = sum(1 for s in items if s.label == "low")
342	
343	    unresolved_high = sum(
344	        1
345	        for i, s in enumerate(items)
346	        if s.label == "high" and i not in resolved
347	    )
348	    blocking = unresolved_high >= HIGH_BLOCK_COUNT
349	
350	    return SeverityAggregate(
351	        weighted_count=weighted,
352	        max_value=max_v,
353	        max_label=label_for(max_v),
354	        high_count=high,
355	        medium_count=medium,
356	        low_count=low,
357	        objections_total=len(items),
358	        blocking=blocking,
359	        response_required_high=high > 0,
360	        response_recommended_medium=medium > 0,
361	    )
362	
363	
364	# ── MQS coupling ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
365	
366	
367	def mqs_severity_penalty(agg: SeverityAggregate) -> float:
368	    """Return a multiplicative penalty in (0, 1] for the MQS Severity sub-score.
369	
370	    Two high-severity objections (the blocking-gate threshold) take the
371	    sub-score below the publish threshold — concretely, multiplied by
372	    0.5 so a strong base severity falls under the typical 0.6 publish
373	    bar. Ten low-severity objections do not (their weighted count is
374	    low and the max is in the low bracket).
375	    """
376	
377	    if agg.objections_total == 0:
378	        return 1.0
379	
380	    # Cap the weighted-count drag at 0.85 so even a heavy-but-low pile
381	    # cannot zero the sub-score on its own; high-severity objections
382	    # still need to fire the max-gate to drop below publish.
383	    drag = max(0.0, 1.0 - 0.05 * agg.weighted_count)
384	    drag = max(drag, 0.85)
385	
386	    if agg.blocking:
387	        return 0.5 * drag
388	    if agg.high_count >= 1:
389	        # Single high without the blocking threshold — meaningful but
390	        # not fatal, so a softer multiplier.
391	        return 0.7 * drag
392	    if agg.medium_count >= 2:
393	        return 0.85 * drag
394	    return drag
395	
396	
397	# ── Cascade-graph helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────
398	
399	
400	class _CascadeReader(Protocol):
401	    """Minimal cascade interface this module needs.
402	
403	    Production wires :class:`noosphere.cascade.graph.CascadeGraph`;
404	    tests pass a stub. We only need the two read paths exposed by
405	    :mod:`noosphere.cascade.traverse`.
406	    """
407	
408	    def explain(self, node_id: str) -> list: ...
409	    def downstream(self, node_id: str) -> list: ...
410	
411	
412	def claim_centrality_from_cascade(
413	    reader: _CascadeReader, claim_node_id: str
414	) -> float:
415	    """Compute a [0,1] centrality for the attacked claim.
416	
417	    Centrality here is operationalised as the *fan-out* of downstream
418	    consumers: how many other claims/conclusions depend on or are
419	    supported by this one. A claim that nothing else rests on has
420	    centrality 0; a claim that 5+ downstreams rely on caps at 1.0.
421	
422	    Kept deliberately simple — we want a deterministic signal the
423	    rubric can apply without an LLM call. Operators can replace the
424	    function later without touching :func:`score_objection`.
425	    """
426	
427	    edges = reader.downstream(claim_node_id)
428	    n = len(edges)
429	    # Saturating function: 0 -> 0, 1 -> 0.4, 2 -> 0.65, 5+ -> ~1.0.
430	    if n <= 0:
431	        return 0.0
432	    return _clamp01(1.0 - (1.0 / (1.0 + 0.6 * n)))
433	
434	
435	def cascade_weight_from_edge_confidence(confidence: float) -> float:
436	    """Pass-through with clamp; named for the rubric input it feeds."""
437	
438	    return _clamp01(confidence)
439	
440	
441	__all__ = [
442	    "HIGH_BLOCK_COUNT",
443	    "LOW_MAX",
444	    "MEDIUM_MAX",
445	    "ObjectionSeverity",
446	    "SeverityAggregate",
447	    "SeverityInputs",
448	    "SeverityLabel",
449	    "aggregate",
450	    "cascade_weight_from_edge_confidence",
451	    "claim_centrality_from_cascade",
452	    "is_stale",
453	    "label_for",
454	    "mark_stale",
455	    "mqs_severity_penalty",
456	    "score_objection",
457	]
458

file.content:
"""Severity scoring for peer-review objections.

Every objection produced by the swarm is annotated with a severity in
``{"low", "medium", "high"}`` and a numeric value in ``[0, 1]``. The
score answers a single question: *if this objection is true, how much
would it move the conclusion's confidence?*

The rubric is composed of five inputs, deliberately mostly grounded
outside the LLM judge so the model cannot self-promote a nitpick into
a structural blow:

1. ``cascade_weight`` — confidence of the supporting edge the objection
   attacks. A 0.9 supports edge under attack matters more than a 0.3 one.
2. ``claim_centrality`` — how central the attacked claim is in the
   conclusion's argument graph (downstream-edge fan-out, normalised).
   An attack on a load-bearing premise outranks one on a tangent.
3. ``failure_mode_severity`` — if the objection points to a curated
   failure mode (see :mod:`noosphere.methods.failure_modes`), the
   curated severity is folded in. Curated `high` failure modes carry
   strong weight because a human approved them.
4. ``source_credibility`` — when the objection cites a source, the
   source's Beta-credibility posterior modulates the weight. A
   tabloid-cited objection cannot land at high.
5. ``judge_severity`` — the LLM judge's own estimate, **capped** by a
   bracket the structural inputs above define. The judge picks within a
   range; it cannot promote past it.

The bracket cap is the discipline: an objection's max severity is set
by the structural inputs; the judge can place inside that bracket but
not above. This is what makes the score robust to model puffery.

A severity score expires when the underlying conclusion is materially
revised (revision engine, prompt 16). Stale scores are not carried
forward — see :func:`is_stale`.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Iterable, Literal, Optional, Protocol

SeverityLabel = Literal["low", "medium", "high"]

# Public bracket boundaries. Documented in the module docstring; the
# tests in `test_objection_severity.py` pin them.
LOW_MAX = 0.34
MEDIUM_MAX = 0.67  # >= MEDIUM_MAX is "high"


def _clamp01(x: float) -> float:
    if x != x:  # NaN
        return 0.0
    return max(0.0, min(1.0, float(x)))


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SeverityInputs:
    """The structural inputs that bound an objection's severity.

    All numeric fields are in [0, 1]. Callers fill what they have; a
    missing input contributes 0 (i.e. nothing) rather than skewing the
    bracket. The judge's estimate is provided separately so the rubric
    can cap it explicitly.
    """

    cascade_weight: float = 0.0
    claim_centrality: float = 0.0
    failure_mode_severity: float = 0.0  # 0=no match, low=0.33, med=0.67, high=1.0
    source_credibility: Optional[float] = None  # None = no source cited
    judge_severity: Optional[float] = None  # None = no LLM estimate


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ObjectionSeverity:
    """The severity annotation attached to one objection."""

    value: float
    label: SeverityLabel
    bracket_floor: float
    bracket_ceiling: float
    inputs: SeverityInputs
    judge_capped: bool
    rationale: str = ""
    scored_at: datetime = field(
        default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    )
    # Set when `mark_stale` runs after a material revision.
    stale: bool = False

    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "value": round(self.value, 4),
            "label": self.label,
            "bracket_floor": round(self.bracket_floor, 4),
            "bracket_ceiling": round(self.bracket_ceiling, 4),
            "judge_capped": self.judge_capped,
            "stale": self.stale,
            "rationale": self.rationale,
            "inputs": {
                "cascade_weight": round(self.inputs.cascade_weight, 4),
                "claim_centrality": round(self.inputs.claim_centrality, 4),
                "failure_mode_severity": round(
                    self.inputs.failure_mode_severity, 4
                ),
                "source_credibility": (
                    None
                    if self.inputs.source_credibility is None
                    else round(self.inputs.source_credibility, 4)
                ),
                "judge_severity": (
                    None
                    if self.inputs.judge_severity is None
                    else round(self.inputs.judge_severity, 4)
                ),
            },
            "scored_at": self.scored_at.isoformat(),
        }


# ── Severity rubric ──────────────────────────────────────────────────


# Weights applied to the structural inputs to compute the bracket
# ceiling. Cascade-weight and centrality are the two primary structural
# drivers — together they account for the bulk of the budget. The
# failure-mode signal is curated, so it carries real weight too. Source
# credibility is a multiplicative modulation when present, not an
# additive driver — a low-credibility source caps the bracket from
# above. The weights are tuned so that:
#   * cascade=1, centrality=1, failure_mode=0 -> ceiling ~0.85 (allows
#     high if the judge sees a structural blow).
#   * cascade=0, centrality=0, failure_mode=0 -> ceiling = 0 (the judge
#     cannot promote a nitpick into anything).
_W_CASCADE = 0.40
_W_CENTRALITY = 0.30
_W_FAILURE = 0.30


def _structural_ceiling(inp: SeverityInputs) -> float:
    """Upper bound on severity from structural inputs alone."""

    raw = (
        _W_CASCADE * _clamp01(inp.cascade_weight)
        + _W_CENTRALITY * _clamp01(inp.claim_centrality)
        + _W_FAILURE * _clamp01(inp.failure_mode_severity)
    )
    # Source credibility only modulates *down*: a present-but-weak
    # source lowers the ceiling; a present-and-strong source leaves it
    # alone; a missing source leaves it alone (we don't penalise an
    # objection for not citing a source — many objections are purely
    # methodological).
    cred = inp.source_credibility
    if cred is not None:
        raw = raw * _clamp01(cred)
    return _clamp01(raw)


def _structural_floor(inp: SeverityInputs) -> float:
    """Minimum severity from structural inputs.

    The floor catches the case where the structural signal is so strong
    (e.g. curated `high` failure mode on a load-bearing claim) that
    even a meek LLM estimate should not pull the score down to "low".
    """

    # The floor is the failure-mode signal when it lands in the high
    # bracket *and* the cascade/centrality structural support is
    # non-trivial. Otherwise no floor — the judge can park anywhere
    # within [0, ceiling].
    structural_support = _clamp01(
        0.5 * inp.cascade_weight + 0.5 * inp.claim_centrality
    )
    if inp.failure_mode_severity >= MEDIUM_MAX and structural_support >= 0.4:
        # Floor at the bottom of the medium bracket — never below
        # `low` if the curated prior is high and the claim has
        # structural weight.
        return LOW_MAX
    return 0.0


def label_for(value: float) -> SeverityLabel:
    if value < LOW_MAX:
        return "low"
    if value < MEDIUM_MAX:
        return "medium"
    return "high"


def score_objection(
    inputs: SeverityInputs, *, rationale: str = ""
) -> ObjectionSeverity:
    """Compute an :class:`ObjectionSeverity` from the rubric inputs.

    The judge's estimate is clamped into ``[floor, ceiling]``. If it is
    None, the severity falls back to the ceiling (the structural inputs
    define the score on their own). This guarantees:

    * Severity is monotonic in ``cascade_weight`` (and centrality, and
      failure-mode severity), holding other inputs fixed — see tests.
    * The judge cannot promote a low-bracket objection into "high".
    * A missing source is treated charitably (no penalty), but a
      present-and-weak source caps the ceiling.
    """

    ceiling = _structural_ceiling(inputs)
    floor = min(_structural_floor(inputs), ceiling)

    judge = inputs.judge_severity
    capped = False
    if judge is None:
        value = ceiling
    else:
        j = _clamp01(judge)
        value = j
        if value > ceiling:
            value = ceiling
            capped = True
        if value < floor:
            value = floor
            # Promoting up to floor is *not* a "judge cap" event — the
            # judge can disagree downwards but the structural prior
            # holds. We still record it on the rationale below.

    return ObjectionSeverity(
        value=value,
        label=label_for(value),
        bracket_floor=floor,
        bracket_ceiling=ceiling,
        inputs=inputs,
        judge_capped=capped,
        rationale=rationale,
    )


def mark_stale(severity: ObjectionSeverity) -> ObjectionSeverity:
    """Return a copy of ``severity`` with ``stale=True``.

    Called by the revision engine when the conclusion is materially
    revised — the old severity is no longer load-bearing because the
    underlying argument graph may have shifted.
    """

    return ObjectionSeverity(
        value=severity.value,
        label=severity.label,
        bracket_floor=severity.bracket_floor,
        bracket_ceiling=severity.bracket_ceiling,
        inputs=severity.inputs,
        judge_capped=severity.judge_capped,
        rationale=severity.rationale,
        scored_at=severity.scored_at,
        stale=True,
    )


def is_stale(severity: ObjectionSeverity) -> bool:
    return severity.stale


# ── Aggregation: weighted count + max-severity gate ─────────────────


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SeverityAggregate:
    """Aggregate of a swarm pass's per-objection severities.

    Fed into the MQS Severity sub-score and the publication gate.
    """

    weighted_count: float
    max_value: float
    max_label: SeverityLabel
    high_count: int
    medium_count: int
    low_count: int
    objections_total: int
    blocking: bool  # >= 2 unresolved high or any single high+blocker
    response_required_high: bool
    response_recommended_medium: bool

    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "weighted_count": round(self.weighted_count, 4),
            "max_value": round(self.max_value, 4),
            "max_label": self.max_label,
            "high_count": self.high_count,
            "medium_count": self.medium_count,
            "low_count": self.low_count,
            "objections_total": self.objections_total,
            "blocking": self.blocking,
            "response_required_high": self.response_required_high,
            "response_recommended_medium": self.response_recommended_medium,
        }


# Two unresolved high-severity objections trip the publication gate.
# The number is intentional — one outlier high might be a single
# reviewer overshooting; two distinct high objections is a structural
# pattern. Documented in tests.
HIGH_BLOCK_COUNT = 2


def aggregate(
    severities: Iterable[ObjectionSeverity],
    *,
    responses_resolved_for: Optional[set[int]] = None,
) -> SeverityAggregate:
    """Aggregate severities into the MQS-feeding signal.

    ``responses_resolved_for`` is an optional set of severity indices
    that have been answered by a reviewer/founder rebuttal. Only
    *unresolved* high severities count toward the blocking gate — the
    response loop is the firm's escape hatch.

    Stale severities are ignored entirely. The revision engine clears
    them by calling :func:`mark_stale`; an aggregate computed after a
    revision treats those as if they were never produced.
    """

    items = [s for s in severities if not s.stale]
    if not items:
        return SeverityAggregate(
            weighted_count=0.0,
            max_value=0.0,
            max_label="low",
            high_count=0,
            medium_count=0,
            low_count=0,
            objections_total=0,
            blocking=False,
            response_required_high=False,
            response_recommended_medium=False,
        )

    resolved = responses_resolved_for or set()
    weighted = sum(s.value for s in items)
    max_v = max(s.value for s in items)
    high = sum(1 for s in items if s.label == "high")
    medium = sum(1 for s in items if s.label == "medium")
    low = sum(1 for s in items if s.label == "low")

    unresolved_high = sum(
        1
        for i, s in enumerate(items)
        if s.label == "high" and i not in resolved
    )
    blocking = unresolved_high >= HIGH_BLOCK_COUNT

    return SeverityAggregate(
        weighted_count=weighted,
        max_value=max_v,
        max_label=label_for(max_v),
        high_count=high,
        medium_count=medium,
        low_count=low,
        objections_total=len(items),
        blocking=blocking,
        response_required_high=high > 0,
        response_recommended_medium=medium > 0,
    )


# ── MQS coupling ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────


def mqs_severity_penalty(agg: SeverityAggregate) -> float:
    """Return a multiplicative penalty in (0, 1] for the MQS Severity sub-score.

    Two high-severity objections (the blocking-gate threshold) take the
    sub-score below the publish threshold — concretely, multiplied by
    0.5 so a strong base severity falls under the typical 0.6 publish
    bar. Ten low-severity objections do not (their weighted count is
    low and the max is in the low bracket).
    """

    if agg.objections_total == 0:
        return 1.0

    # Cap the weighted-count drag at 0.85 so even a heavy-but-low pile
    # cannot zero the sub-score on its own; high-severity objections
    # still need to fire the max-gate to drop below publish.
    drag = max(0.0, 1.0 - 0.05 * agg.weighted_count)
    drag = max(drag, 0.85)

    if agg.blocking:
        return 0.5 * drag
    if agg.high_count >= 1:
        # Single high without the blocking threshold — meaningful but
        # not fatal, so a softer multiplier.
        return 0.7 * drag
    if agg.medium_count >= 2:
        return 0.85 * drag
    return drag


# ── Cascade-graph helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────


class _CascadeReader(Protocol):
    """Minimal cascade interface this module needs.

    Production wires :class:`noosphere.cascade.graph.CascadeGraph`;
    tests pass a stub. We only need the two read paths exposed by
    :mod:`noosphere.cascade.traverse`.
    """

    def explain(self, node_id: str) -> list: ...
    def downstream(self, node_id: str) -> list: ...


def claim_centrality_from_cascade(
    reader: _CascadeReader, claim_node_id: str
) -> float:
    """Compute a [0,1] centrality for the attacked claim.

    Centrality here is operationalised as the *fan-out* of downstream
    consumers: how many other claims/conclusions depend on or are
    supported by this one. A claim that nothing else rests on has
    centrality 0; a claim that 5+ downstreams rely on caps at 1.0.

    Kept deliberately simple — we want a deterministic signal the
    rubric can apply without an LLM call. Operators can replace the
    function later without touching :func:`score_objection`.
    """

    edges = reader.downstream(claim_node_id)
    n = len(edges)
    # Saturating function: 0 -> 0, 1 -> 0.4, 2 -> 0.65, 5+ -> ~1.0.
    if n <= 0:
        return 0.0
    return _clamp01(1.0 - (1.0 / (1.0 + 0.6 * n)))


def cascade_weight_from_edge_confidence(confidence: float) -> float:
    """Pass-through with clamp; named for the rubric input it feeds."""

    return _clamp01(confidence)


__all__ = [
    "HIGH_BLOCK_COUNT",
    "LOW_MAX",
    "MEDIUM_MAX",
    "ObjectionSeverity",
    "SeverityAggregate",
    "SeverityInputs",
    "SeverityLabel",
    "aggregate",
    "cascade_weight_from_edge_confidence",
    "claim_centrality_from_cascade",
    "is_stale",
    "label_for",
    "mark_stale",
    "mqs_severity_penalty",
    "score_objection",
]

1	"""
2	Belief revision over the cascade graph.
3	
4	This is a first concrete step toward an AGM-style revision primitive:
5	when new evidence arrives that contradicts (or supports) a published
6	claim, we want to compute the minimal-distance update to the firm's
7	belief set rather than simply editing a conclusion in place.
8	
9	The algorithm here is a *single-shot propagator*: given a set of
10	``RevisionInput`` records (claim_id + new evidence text + signed
11	evidence weight), it walks forward through the ``supports`` /
12	``refines`` (REFORMULATES) / ``instantiates`` (SPECIALIZES) /
13	``contradicts`` relations, recomputes the composite confidence of every
14	reachable conclusion node, and emits a ``RevisionPlan`` describing the
15	diff. The plan is the *preview*; committing it appends a
16	``RevisionEvent`` to the audit ledger and applies the new edge
17	confidences. Reverting the revision pops the most recent event.
18	
19	Determinism contract
20	--------------------
21	Same graph snapshot + same inputs → byte-identical plan. We achieve
22	this by:
23	- iterating edges in a stable (relation, src, dst, edge_id) order,
24	- breaking ties between plans by lexicographic conclusion id,
25	- never sampling, never reading wall-clock time inside scoring,
26	- recording the edge_ids consulted into the plan so a replay can
27	  detect graph drift.
28	
29	What this is NOT (yet)
30	----------------------
31	- Not a full AGM revision: we don't pick among multiple maximal
32	  consistent subsets — instead we pick the *single* propagation that
33	  minimises the number of downstream conclusions whose confidence
34	  shifts more than δ. The "minimum-distance principle" here is a
35	  cardinality minimisation over the cascade frontier, documented in
36	  ``METHODOLOGICAL_REORIENTATION.md``.
37	- A revision NEVER deletes a claim. Retracting a claim is a separate
38	  operation (``retract_claim``) that produces its own RevisionEvent
39	  with a sentinel weight of -1.0.
40	"""
41	
42	from __future__ import annotations
43	
44	import json
45	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
46	from datetime import datetime, timezone
47	from typing import Iterable, Optional, Protocol
48	from uuid import uuid4
49	
50	from noosphere.models import (
51	    CascadeEdge,
52	    CascadeEdgeRelation,
53	    CascadeNodeKind,
54	)
55	
56	
57	# ── tunables ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
58	# Defaults; callers can override per-call. These thresholds are public so
59	# the UI can show the same numbers it gates on.
60	
61	DEFAULT_DELTA = 0.05      # ignore confidence shifts smaller than this
62	DEFAULT_THETA = 0.30      # below this composite confidence, "newly contradicted"
63	DEFAULT_MAX_AUTOCOMMIT = 12  # K — UI requires typed confirmation above this
64	
65	# Per-relation weights applied when composing confidence. Positive
66	# relations add support; CONTRADICTS / REFUTES subtract. Tuned so a
67	# direct SUPPORTS edge dominates a SPECIALIZES edge of the same raw
68	# confidence — i.e. a claim that *directly* supports a conclusion
69	# matters more than one that merely refines it.
70	_RELATION_WEIGHT: dict[CascadeEdgeRelation, float] = {
71	    CascadeEdgeRelation.SUPPORTS: 1.0,
72	    CascadeEdgeRelation.EXTRACTED_FROM: 1.0,
73	    CascadeEdgeRelation.AGGREGATES: 1.0,
74	    CascadeEdgeRelation.DEPENDS_ON: 1.0,
75	    CascadeEdgeRelation.REFORMULATES: 0.7,   # "refines"
76	    CascadeEdgeRelation.SPECIALIZES: 0.7,    # "instantiates"
77	    CascadeEdgeRelation.GENERALIZES: 0.5,
78	    CascadeEdgeRelation.COHERES_WITH: 0.5,
79	    CascadeEdgeRelation.PREDICTS: 0.5,
80	    CascadeEdgeRelation.REFUTES: -1.0,
81	    CascadeEdgeRelation.CONTRADICTS: -1.0,
82	}
83	
84	# Relations that count as evidence flow for revision propagation.
85	# Contradicts/refutes are included so a flip on a contradicting claim
86	# also propagates.
87	_PROPAGATING_RELATIONS: frozenset[CascadeEdgeRelation] = frozenset(
88	    {
89	        CascadeEdgeRelation.SUPPORTS,
90	        CascadeEdgeRelation.REFORMULATES,
91	        CascadeEdgeRelation.SPECIALIZES,
92	        CascadeEdgeRelation.EXTRACTED_FROM,
93	        CascadeEdgeRelation.AGGREGATES,
94	        CascadeEdgeRelation.DEPENDS_ON,
95	        CascadeEdgeRelation.REFUTES,
96	        CascadeEdgeRelation.CONTRADICTS,
97	    }
98	)
99	
100	
101	# ── public dataclasses ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
102	
103	
104	@dataclass(frozen=True)
105	class RevisionInput:
106	    """A single piece of new evidence targeting an existing claim.
107	
108	    ``weight`` is signed and clamped to [-1.0, 1.0]:
109	        +1 = the evidence fully corroborates the targeted claim,
110	         0 = neutral / informational,
111	        -1 = the evidence fully contradicts the targeted claim.
112	    """
113	
114	    claim_id: str
115	    new_evidence: str
116	    weight: float
117	
118	    def clamped_weight(self) -> float:
119	        return max(-1.0, min(1.0, self.weight))
120	
121	
122	@dataclass(frozen=True)
123	class ConfidenceShift:
124	    """Per-conclusion before/after confidence and the classification."""
125	
126	    conclusion_id: str
127	    before: float
128	    after: float
129	    classification: str  # "changed" | "newly_contradicted" | "newly_supported" | "stable"
130	
131	    @property
132	    def delta(self) -> float:
133	        return self.after - self.before
134	
135	
136	@dataclass(frozen=True)
137	class RevisionPlan:
138	    """The dry-run output of ``compute_revision``.
139	
140	    Splits the impact into three buckets so the UI modal can render them
141	    separately. ``stable_count`` is reported because the operator UX
142	    contract is "show every conclusion that would change *and* the count
143	    of those that wouldn't" so the founder can see the blast radius is
144	    bounded.
145	    """
146	
147	    plan_id: str
148	    inputs: tuple[RevisionInput, ...]
149	    changed: tuple[ConfidenceShift, ...]
150	    newly_contradicted: tuple[ConfidenceShift, ...]
151	    newly_supported: tuple[ConfidenceShift, ...]
152	    stable_count: int
153	    consulted_edge_ids: tuple[str, ...]
154	    delta: float
155	    theta: float
156	
157	    @property
158	    def affected_count(self) -> int:
159	        return (
160	            len(self.changed)
161	            + len(self.newly_contradicted)
162	            + len(self.newly_supported)
163	        )
164	
165	    def requires_typed_confirmation(self, k: int = DEFAULT_MAX_AUTOCOMMIT) -> bool:
166	        return self.affected_count > k
167	
168	    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
169	        return {
170	            "plan_id": self.plan_id,
171	            "inputs": [
172	                {"claim_id": i.claim_id, "weight": i.clamped_weight(), "new_evidence": i.new_evidence}
173	                for i in self.inputs
174	            ],
175	            "changed": [_shift_to_dict(s) for s in self.changed],
176	            "newly_contradicted": [_shift_to_dict(s) for s in self.newly_contradicted],
177	            "newly_supported": [_shift_to_dict(s) for s in self.newly_supported],
178	            "stable_count": self.stable_count,
179	            "consulted_edge_ids": list(self.consulted_edge_ids),
180	            "delta": self.delta,
181	            "theta": self.theta,
182	        }
183	
184	
185	def _shift_to_dict(s: ConfidenceShift) -> dict:
186	    return {
187	        "conclusion_id": s.conclusion_id,
188	        "before": s.before,
189	        "after": s.after,
190	        "delta": s.delta,
191	        "classification": s.classification,
192	    }
193

195	@dataclass(frozen=True)
196	class RevisionEvent:
197	    """Audit-trail record for a committed revision.
198	
199	    The ``pre_confidence_snapshot`` is what time-replay needs: the
200	    confidence values *before* the revision applied, keyed by
201	    conclusion id. Replaying the ledger up to (but not including) this
202	    event reconstructs the prior belief state exactly.
203	    """
204	
205	    event_id: str
206	    committed_at: datetime
207	    inputs: tuple[RevisionInput, ...]

209	    pre_confidence_snapshot: dict[str, float]

214	            "event_id": self.event_id,
